type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (14-20) is reporting NOx, CO, VOC, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2014. This facility reported NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2013. The following changes occurred from 2013 to 2014, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased by 85%
2) CO emissions increased by 106%
3) VOC emissions increased by 30%
4) PM10 emissions increased by 46%
5) PM2.5 emissions increased by 46%
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added new equipment in December 2013, which was operational for the entire year in 2014: four (4) 1,480 hp Waukesha compressor engines, two (2) 1,000 hp Caterpillar compressor engines, one (1) 1,135 Waukesha generator engine, 20 line heaters and one boiler.
2) Flaring volume increased from 1,381.3 e3m3 to 1915.8 e3m3.
3) Increase in production for 2014.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (14-20) is reporting NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2013. This facility reported NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2012. The following changes occurred from 2012 to 2013, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased from 103.52 to 137.73 tonnes (+33%)
2) CO emissions increased from 77.38 to 93.34 tonnes (+21%)
3) PM10 emissions increased from 0.59 to 3.88 tonnes (+562%)
4) PM2.5 emissions increased from 0.59 to 3.88  tonnes (+562%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added new equipment in December 2013: four (4) 1,480 hp Waukesha compressor engines, two (2) 1,000 hp Caterpillar compressor engines, one (1) 1,135 Waukesha generator engine, 20 line heaters and one boiler.
2) Flaring volume increased from 116.3 e3m3 to 1,381.3 e3m3.
3) Increase in production.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (14-20) is reporting NOx, CO2, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2012. This facility reported NOx and CO2 emissions for 2011. The following changes occured from 2011 to 2012, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased from 94.13 to 103.52 tonnes (+10%)
2) CO2 emissions increased from 57.14 to 77.38 tonnes (+35%)
3) PM10 emissions increased from 0.26 to 0.59 tonnes (+125%)
4) PM2.5 emissions increased from 0.26 to 0.59  tonnes (+125%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Increased run time on all five (5) engines and all 12 heaters;
2) Three (3) 1,480 hp engines operated at an increased power during 2012 compared to 2011.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The NOx emissions calculations have been reviewed for 2020. We confirm that the reported quantities calculated based on the 2020 Petrinex data as well as equipment specs and operating hours provided by Tourmaline are correct.
Emissions factors were taken from either engine manufacturer’s specification sheets, engine tests, or published documents by Clearstone Engineering Ltd.

The Wildriver 14-20 Gas Plant had higher emissions in comparison to other facilities in the same NAICS code because this Gas Plant has 12 large engines (@1500 hp) generating emissions. Other facilities with the same NAICS code typically have three to six large engines.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The CO emissions calculations have been reviewed for 2020. We confirm that the reported quantities calculated based on the 2020 Petrinex data as well as equipment specs and operating hours provided by Tourmaline are correct.
Emissions factors were taken from either engine manufacturer’s specification sheets, engine tests, or published documents by Clearstone Engineering Ltd.

The Wildriver 14-20 Gas Plant had higher emissions in comparison to other facilities in the same NAICS code because this Gas Plant has 12 large engines (@1500 hp) generating emissions. Other facilities with the same NAICS code typically have three to six large engines.
